title: Lamentation
program_note:
Premiere: January 8, 1930, Maxine Elliott's Theatre, New York City.
Lamentation is a "dance of sorrow." It is not the sorrow of a specific person, time, or place, but the personification of grief itself.

title: Nobody 01100011
program_note:
1: a duet of contrived connection, 2: a duet of deceptive expertise, 3: a duet of hallucinations.
This work was crafted with the use of AI systems including ChatGPT, and "Archive" & "AISoma" by Studio Wayne McGregor & Google Arts & Culture.

title: Studies in Schmaltz
program_note: After an illustrious musical career in Vienna, violinist Hugo Gottesmann immigrated to the United States in 1936. He was a member of the renowned Busch Quartet, concertmaster of the Fort Wayne Philharmonic, and resident violinist for 28 years at the Bay View College of Music in Michigan. During my idyllic summers spent in Bay View between the ages of 10-15, I took my weekly violin lesson with Hugo and listened to him perform in recitals and at Sunday Vespers. His thick accent, garlic-rich breath and "Schmaltzy" style of playing left an indelible impression on me. 60 years later, I offer this affectionate tribute to his artistry as a set of kitschy solo dances that look back to an era of refined decadence in Europe between wars.
